source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / Other Lifting

event.top_event: Pressurized hose whip causing personnel injury
actions_taken: Both injured personnel medevaced to New Orleans University Hospital
Injured personnel released from hospital on Feb 14 and returned to work on February 19
Extra whip check installed beneath deck penetration valve after incident
BSEE Accident Investigation Team arrived on location on February 14, 2024
event.summary: On February 13, 2024, at approximately 22:35 hours, two Tetra filtration crew members were injured on the Transocean Deepwater Proteus drillship during completion operations on the SS005 well. While attempting to repair a small leak in a 6-inch camlock connection, the Tetra employee released the camlock levers. Pressurized fluid in the hose caused the hose to blow off the deck flange and strike two personnel. One worker received facial impact and a fractured tooth; the second worker was struck and fell against a pipe, sustaining bruising and chemical exposure.
recommendations: Ensure proper PPE is worn during operations involving pressurized lines and chemical exposure, including chemical-resistant clothing and gloves in addition to eye protection
Implement hazard identification procedures to identify trapped pressure risks during line flushing operations
Establish and enforce procedures to completely drain and bleed pressure from lines before disconnecting hose connections
Install whip checks around all camlock connections being disconnected to prevent hose whip injuries
Provide adequate training and supervision for short-service and inexperienced personnel working with pressurized systems

threats.name: Trapped pressure in disconnected hose line
Failure to bleed pressure before camlock disconnection
Inadequate whip check protection on camlock connection

consequences.name: Facial impact and fractured tooth injury
Thigh/femur impact, bruising, and chemical exposure

controls.name: Job Safety and Environmental Analysis (JSEA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and required controls

supporting_text: There was a Job Safety and Environmental Analysis (JSEA) used for this operation, and it was signed off by the Tetra filtration employees. In the JSEA, there was a checklist of items checked off that referenced: chemical exposure and PPE pressure, and whip checks, bleed off lines before removing, and reviewing the MSDS.
The JSEA collected states: Drain lines before opening camlock connections and bleed off lines before removing hoses.

controls.name: Pressure bleed-off procedure before hose disconnection
controls.barrier_role: Operational procedure to relieve trapped pressure

supporting_text: It was found during the investigation that it is common practice when flushing deck penetration lines, and the pump shuts off, that you wait for the fluid pressure to leave the lines before closing the valves. In this case, that didn't happen, and the deck valve was immediately closed once pumping was finished. This resulted in trapped pressure in the line that was being disconnected.
The deck penetration lines that were flushed were not completely drained and the pressure was not bled before being disconnected.

controls.name: Whip checks on hose connections
controls.barrier_role: Physical restraint to prevent hose whip injury

supporting_text: There were whip checks on the 6 inch hoses, but not around the camlock connection being disconnected, to prevent the hose that was being removed from knocking the two injured employees down.
After the incident concluded, and the 6 inch camlock hose was reconnected, there was an extra whip installed. This whip check was attached beneath the deck penetration valve and to the hose.

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) - Eye and body protection
controls.barrier_role: Personal protection against chemical exposure and impact
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense for personnel safety

supporting_text: The appropriate PPE referenced in the MSDS states requirement of eye protection: wear safety glasses with splash shield or safety goggles/shield and Clothing: wear appropriate chemical resistant clothing and gloves. It was found that only safety glasses were worn during this operation and the chemical resistant clothing or gloves were not worn.

Not wearing the proper PPE (The MSDS states; Eye Protection: wear safety glasses with splash shield or safety goggles/shield and Clothing: wear appropriate chemical resistant clothing and gloves). Only safety glasses were worn during this operation.

controls.name: Communication and line-up verification between Derrick hand and Tetra crew
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to ensure correct valve alignment and pressure status

supporting_text: The Transocean Derrick hand stated the valves were lined up to send drill water to the Tetra filter unit, and that he double verified the line-ups. He then communicated with the Tetra crew by radio that the valves were lined up.
The Derrick hand then checked his line-up on the in-board line, and Tetra checked their line-up.

controls.name: Supervision and hazard awareness oversight
controls.barrier_role: Management control to ensure hazard identification and proper work practices

supporting_text: Supervision: Not ensuring the use of the proper PPE. The JSEA reviewed for this operation specified the use of PPE for chemical exposure.
Hazard identification was not appropriately done, hazards with the flushing operations and trapped pressured were not identified or followed.

controls.name: Personnel competence and training for pressurized line operations
controls.barrier_role: Organizational control to ensure personnel are qualified for task

supporting_text: In the Tetra flushing operations, inexperienced personnel were involved in the incident. BSEE found that one of the Tetra filtration crew members was a short service employee.
Inexperience Offshore: Short service employee involved in the incident.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
